These are known layout artifacts from PDF-to-text conversion and are handled by a separate deterministic form-feed pass. Examples: (1) isolated page number '5' appears between 'physical' and 'control' in paragraph [¶16]; (2) isolated '6' appears before section marker 'IV' in paragraph boundary; (3) trailing '9' at end of opinion. These are FORM-FEED ARTIFACTS per rule 8 and should not be fixed here.

flags.location_quote: [¶1] Karley Anderson appeals from an order denying her motion to modify residential responsibility and from a contempt order awarding attorney's fees to Seth Pedie. Anderson argues the district court erred by concluding she failed to establish a prima facie case for modification entitling her to an evidentiary hearing, and awarding attorney's fees in excess of the amount requested. Pedie requests sanctions against Anderson for violating the North Dakota Rules of Appellate Procedure. We affirm the order denying Anderson's motion to modify residential responsibility and the contempt order awarding attorney's fees, and deny sanctions on appeal. I
flags.description: Section marker 'I' embedded at end of paragraph text (line 37). Should be a separate centered section header. Character corruption of Roman numeral markers.

flags.location_quote: [¶3] In October 2020, Pedie moved the court for an order finding Anderson in contempt, arguing she had violated various provisions of the judgment. He requested attorney's fees in the amount of $1,500 as a sanction. In December 2020, Anderson moved to modify primary residential responsibility, contending Pedie neglected W.D.P.'s physical and emotional health and developmental needs. The district court denied Anderson's motion to modify primary residential responsibility without an evidentiary hearing, concluding she failed to make a prima facie case for modification. The court found Anderson in contempt of court for failing to notify Pedie of medical appointments and providing false allegations to medical professionals of harm to W.D.P. The court awarded Pedie attorney's fees in the amount of $2,000. Π
flags.description: Section marker 'Π' (Greek pi, U+03A0) embedded at end of paragraph text (line 41). Should be 'II' as a separate centered section header. Character corruption of Roman numeral II.

flags.location_quote: [¶10] We conclude that Anderson's assertions do not create prima facie evidence that W.D.P.'s environment under Pedie's care is endangering his physical or emotional health or impairing his emotional development. Ш
flags.description: Section marker 'Ш' (Cyrillic sha, U+0428) embedded at end of paragraph text (line 56). Should be 'III' as a separate centered section header. Character corruption of Roman numeral III.

flags.location_quote: [¶13] Pedie requests sanctions on appeal against Anderson for violating N.D.R.App.P. 30 (appendix to the briefs) and N.D.R.App.P. 31 (filing and service of briefs). Under N.D.R.App.P. 13, we "may take appropriate action against any person failing to perform an act required by rule or court order." "Whether to administer sanctions under N.D.R.App.P. 13 for noncompliance with the Rules of Appellate Procedure is discretionary with this Court." Krump-Wootton v. Krump, 2019 ND 275, ¶ 7, 935 N.W.2d 534. We have examined the alleged violations of the appellate rules, and we decline to exercise our discretion to award sanctions on appeal. V
flags.description: Section marker 'V' embedded at end of paragraph text (line 62). Should be 'IV' as a separate centered section header. This is a formatting artifact where the section heading has been incorrectly attached to paragraph text.

proposals.old_exact: Unjust enrichment requires: impoverishment; (3) a connection between the enrichment and the impoverishment; (4) an absence of justification for the enrichment (1) an enrichment; and impoverishment; and (5) an absence of remedy provided by law.
